props.command: CAR-T Solid Tumor Barrier — Why It Cures Leukemia but Stalls at Solid Tumors
props.segment: CAR-T Solid Tumor Barrier — Why It Cures Leukemia but Stalls at Solid Tumors
output: CAR-T Solid Tumor Barrier — Why It Cures Leukemia but Stalls at Solid Tumors

beats.narration_text: CAR-T achieves 70 to 90 percent complete response in relapsed pediatric B-cell leukemia — a disease that was once terminal. The same engineering approach barely registers response rates in lung, breast, or pancreatic cancer. The cells are built the same way. The problem is entirely in what they face when they arrive.

Research CAR-T therapy in solid tumors versus hematologic cancers: FDA-approved indications with objective response rates, five main barriers to solid tumor activity, and evidence each barrier can be overcome.

props.command: claude "Research CAR-T therapy in solid tumors vs hematologic cancers: list FDA-approved indications with objective response rates, identify the five main barriers to solid tumor activity (antigen heterogeneity, T cell exhaustion, immunosuppressive TME, poor trafficking, on-target off-tumor toxicity), and for each barrier cite the strongest preclinical or clinical evidence it can be overcome."

beats.narration_text: Python script comparing objective response rate data across hematologic versus solid tumor indications.

props.code:
DATA = [
    ("B-ALL (pediatric)", "hematologic", 85, "APPROVED"),
    ("DLBCL",             "hematologic", 40, "APPROVED"),
    ("Multiple Myeloma",  "hematologic", 73, "APPROVED"),
    ("GBM",               "solid",        8, "INVESTIGATIONAL"),
    ("Pancreatic",        "solid",        3, "INVESTIGATIONAL"),
    ("Ovarian",           "solid",        5, "INVESTIGATIONAL"),
]
print(f"{'Indication':<22} {'Type':<14} {'ORR':>5}  {'Status'}")
print("-" * 55)
for name, typ, orr, status in DATA:
    flag = "  <- FLAG" if orr < 15 else ""
    print(f"{name:<22} {typ:<14} {orr:>4}%  {status}{flag}")
props.title: cart_compare.py

beats.narration_text: Bar chart showing CAR-T objective response rates across hematologic and solid tumor indications.
For each of the five barriers, what is the most promising engineering solution currently in clinical trials, with trial ID and phase?

props.command: claude "For each of the five barriers, what is the single most promising engineering solution currently in clinical trials? Cite the trial ID and phase."

beats.narration_text: Barrier list with engineering solution callouts and trial phase status.

The five barriers are separate unsolved engineering problems. A CAR-T that beats antigen heterogeneity still faces exhaustion. Progress requires solving all five. The field is solving them independently — a combination therapy will be needed.
Read the June lab dual-targeting CAR-T papers on CD19 plus CD22. Then read the Pan et al. 2022 armored CAR-T work — what the TME does to a standard versus an IL-15-secreting CAR.
